title: When failover isn’t safe: Building high-availability PostgreSQL on Kubernetes
summary: During a reliability gameday, Datadog engineers discovered that their PostgreSQL clusters couldn’t safely fail over. Here’s how the team redesigned them for high availability using Patroni and synchronous replication.

title: From single pull requests to full software packages: Detecting malicious code at scale
summary: By combining stacked LLM evaluations with tool-driven investigation, we scaled malicious code detection from pull requests to dependency packages without sacrificing accuracy or cost control.

title: Steganography at scale: Embedding share URLs in Datadog widget screenshots
summary: Learn how we embed widget metadata into screenshots using invisible, resilient watermarks, enabling self-describing visualizations at scale.

title: How we built a real-world evaluation platform for autonomous SRE agents at scale
summary: Find out how we built a scalable evaluation platform for Datadog’s Bits AI SRE agent that replays real incidents, detects regressions, and measures agent performance across production scenarios.

title: When upserts don’t update but still write: Debugging Postgres performance at scale
summary: When a high-volume upsert doubled disk writes, Datadog engineers traced the issue to Postgres WAL behavior and rewrote the query to eliminate hidden costs.

title: When an AI agent came knocking: Catching malicious contributions in Datadog’s open source repos
summary: Learn how Datadog detected and resolved issues from hackerbot-claw, an AI-powered automated attack campaign.

title: Designing MCP tools for agents: Lessons from building Datadog’s MCP server
summary: We share lessons learned building Datadog’s MCP server, from designing agent-friendly tools and managing context windows to using queries instead of raw data retrieval.

title: How we reduced the size of our Agent Go binaries by up to 77%
summary: Get an inside look at shrinking large Go binaries in the Datadog Agent through dependency and linker analysis.

title: Hardening eBPF for runtime security: Lessons from Datadog Workload Protection
summary: Learn field-tested lessons for eBPF-powered workload protection.

title: Scaling real-time file monitoring with eBPF: How we filtered billions of kernel events per minute
summary: Learn how Datadog scaled eBPF-powered file monitoring to handle more than 10 billion kernel events per minute while preserving full detection coverage.

title: Replication redefined: How we built a low-latency, multi-tenant data replication platform
summary: Discover how Datadog engineered a scalable Change Data Capture (CDC) platform to replicate data across systems in near real time—reducing search latency by 87%, increasing availability, and powering diverse, multi-tenant use cases across the company.

title: Detecting malicious pull requests at scale with LLMs
summary: Learn how Datadog’s SDLC Security team built an LLM-powered system to detect malicious pull requests at scale—without sacrificing developer velocity.

title: Failure is inevitable: Learning from a large outage, and building for reliability in depth at Datadog
summary: After a major outage, we re-architected Datadog systems to degrade gracefully under failure. Here’s what we learned—and how we’re building forward.

title: Inside Husky’s query engine: Real-time access to 100 trillion events
summary: See how Husky enables interactive querying across 100 trillion events daily by combining caching, smart indexing, and query pruning.

title: From hand-tuned Go to self-optimizing code: Building BitsEvolve
summary: Learn how we turned hot-path optimizations into a system for continuous, AI-assisted performance improvements and saved thousands of cores in the process.

title: Scaling down to speed up: How we improved efficiency of live process metrics by 100x
summary: We re-architected the real-time data pipeline for Datadog’s Processes and Containers views—cutting traffic by 100x and infrastructure use by 98%. This post explores the system challenges, architectural changes, and their impact.

title: Evolving our real-time timeseries storage again: Built in Rust for performance at scale
summary: Discover how we reengineered our metrics storage engine for massive scale with Rust, a shard-per-core model, and real-time performance.

title: How Go 1.24’s Swiss Tables saved us hundreds of gigabytes
summary: Go 1.24’s Swiss Tables cut our map memory usage by up to 70% in high-traffic workloads. Here’s how we profiled the savings and improved performance.

title: How we tracked down a Go 1.24 memory regression across hundreds of pods
summary: We rolled out Go 1.24 and saw a memory regression. Here’s how we dug into system metrics, uncovered a bug in the runtime allocator, and worked with the Go team to help fix it.

title: How we built a real-time, client-side noise suppression library without server dependencies
summary: Learn how we implemented and open-sourced a noise filter for real-time audio chat without compromising performance. Better yet, try the demo and add it to your own project today.

title: How we built reliable log delivery to thousands of unpredictable endpoints
summary: Learn how we built Datadog’s Log Forwarding system for low-latency, high-throughput delivery to thousands of unreliable third-party endpoints.

title: How we scaled fast, reliable configuration distribution to thousands of workload containers
summary: Learn how Datadog engineered a highly reliable, low-latency system to distribute per-tenant configuration data across thousands of containers, enabling real-time log processing at scale.

title: Breaking up a monolith: How we’re unwinding a shared database at scale
summary: Learn how Datadog is breaking up a shared production database at scale—defining clear ownership boundaries, minimizing migration risk, and building the tooling to make decoupling safe, automated, and sustainable.

title: Detecting faulty deployments: Our journey from unlabeled data to supervised learning
summary: Learn how we developed Datadog Automatic Faulty Deployment Detection and improved precision, recall, and time to detection along the way.

title: Squeezing every millisecond: How we rebuilt the Datadog Lambda Extension in Rust
summary: Learn how we rewrote the Datadog Lambda Extension in Rust, cutting cold starts by 82 percent, shrinking the binary 87 percent, and slashing memory use without sacrificing observability.
